sources.name: Sport England
sources.dataset: Active Lives Children and Young People Survey

metadata.methodology: Active Lives data is drawn from Sport England's Active Lives Adult Survey, which measures activity levels among adults aged 16+ in England. 'Active' is defined as achieving 150+ minutes of moderate intensity activity per week (CMO guidelines). 'Inactive' is defined as fewer than 30 minutes per week. The survey uses a random household sampling methodology with approximately 180,000 respondents per year.
knownIssues: The Active Lives Survey replaced the Active People Survey in 2016 — pre-2016 data is not directly comparable
Self-reported activity is known to overestimate actual physical activity levels by 30-50%
The pandemic years (2020-21) saw significant methodology changes including online-only data collection, which may affect comparability
The survey covers England only — separate surveys exist for Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland with different methodologies
